concepts.title: Rutherford's Atomic Model
concepts.description: Rutherford's atomic model is also known as the planetary model of the atom. In this model, the nucleus is at the center and electrons orbit around it.

concepts.title: Electron Spin
concepts.description: Electrons have a property called spin. The spin of an electron is quantized, meaning it can have only two values: +1/2 and -1/2. This property is responsible for the magnetic moment of the electron.

concepts.title: Energy Levels
concepts.description: Electrons in an atom are arranged in different energy levels or shells. The energy level of an electron is quantized, meaning it can have only specific values. The energy level of an electron is denoted by the principal quantum number (n).

concepts.title: Bohr's Model
concepts.description: To remember the features of Bohr's atomic model, use the phrase 'N-Electrons in N-Shells have N-Energy levels'.

concepts.title: Quantization of Energy
concepts.description: Students often confuse the quantization of energy with the continuous nature of energy. However, in the context of atomic physics, energy is quantized, meaning it can have only specific values.

concepts.title: Planck's Constant
concepts.description: Planck's constant (h) is a fundamental constant in physics with a value of 6.626 x 10^-34 J s. It is used to calculate the energy of a photon.

concepts.title: Pauli's Exclusion Principle
concepts.description: Pauli's exclusion principle states that no two electrons in an atom can have the same set of four quantum numbers. This principle explains the arrangement of electrons in different energy levels.

concepts.title: De-Broglie Wavelength
concepts.description: The de-Broglie wavelength of a particle is inversely proportional to its momentum. This property is used to explain the wave-particle duality of matter.
